summary:
Improper pre-op management, including underdosing antithyroid meds or lacking adequate iodine intake, greatly raises the likelihood of developing thyroid storm due to suboptimal suppression of hyperthyroidism prior to surgical intervention.
```

**Final Summary:** 
Improper pre-surgical management such as inadequately controlled thyroxin levels via low doses of antithyroid drugs or insufficient iodine intake substantially elevates the chance of experiencing thyroid storm by failing to adequately suppress hyperthyroid conditions leading up to an operation. ```plaintext
